name: Chocolate Leaves - Leaves you wanting for more:)
directions: Break the chocolate into pieces.
Keep aside in a bowl.
Heat water in a pan.
Place the bowl with chocolate in it making sure that water does not spill into the bowl.
Double boil it until the chocolate melts.
Line a tray with grease-proof paper.
Gently dip the back of each bay leaf in the chocolate and place it on the tray with the chocolate side of the leaf on top.
Chill the leaves for 30 minutes and peel off the bay leaves to make yum chocolate leaves.

name: Homemade Poultry Seasoning
directions: Mix all ingredients thoroughly.
Store in a clean used spice bottle.
tightly covered.

name: Baking Spice - Copycat Pampered Chef Cinnamon Plus Mix
directions: In a medium bowl, combine all ingredients.
Store in an air tight container.
Use this mix in recipes that call for the ingredients above.
I substitute this for all of my cinnamon needs.
If a recipe calls for the spices add up all of the measurments and just put in the same amount of this blend.
It tastes great used in cinnamon and sugar for toast and in cinnamon rolls.

name: Herb Blend For Vegetables
directions: Combine all in a container with a tight fitting lid.

name: Beau Monde Seasoning-Clone Recipe
directions: Mix well and store in an air tight jar.

name: Bell's Poultry/Stuffing Seasoning
directions: Mix ingredients and store in an airtight container.
Sprinkle on poultry before roasting or make a stuffing.
Stuffing: Melt 4 tbls. butter.
Saute 1/3 c.minced onion and 1/2 cup chopped celery until tender.
Salt to taste.
Add vegetables to eight slices of bread (cubed and toasted).
Add 1/2 cup water and 1 1/2 tsp of poultry seasoning to the bread mixture and toss.

name: Wilt Chamberlain's Texas Chili Seasoning
directions: In a small bowl combine all ingredients and mix together well.
Store tightly covered in refrigerator.
Makes about 3 tablespoons.
Can be easily doubled or tripled.

name: Julia Child's Spice Blend
directions: Mix all ingredients well.
Store in a screwtop glass jar.
(I use a funnel to get the spices in the jar).

similar.name: Baking Spice - Copycat Pampered Chef Cinnamon Plus Mix

name: Baking Spice Blend Mix
directions: In a small bowl, combine all ingredients with a wire whisk until thoroughly blended.
Pour mixture into a 1/2 cup container with tight-fitting lid, label and date.

similar.name: Baking Spice - Copycat Pampered Chef Cinnamon Plus Mix

name: Herbes De Provence
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Mix well and spoon into a tightly-lidded jar.
Store in cool, dark place up to 4 months.

name: Poultry Seasoning
directions: Mix together.
Store in an airtight container.

name: Low Sodium Steak Rub
directions: Mix all.
Rub generously on steak or chops.
Enjoy.

name: Lazy Garam Masala
directions: Combine in food processor.

name: Healing Chai Mixture
directions: Mix all the ingredients into either milk, tea (my personal favorite), or any other beverage.
Heat until it comes to a boil and pour the beverage into a mug.
Add honey or sugar into your drink if desired.
Enjoy!

name: Holiday Spice Rub for Poultry
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl.
Loosen skin on chicken or turkey and rub between skin and meat.
Store in a tightly covered jar.

name: Baharat Spice Blend
directions: Combine all the ingredients together till well mixed.
Store in an airtight jar and keep away from direct sunlight.

name: North African Spice Rub
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Store in a sealed container.

name: Perfectly Spiced Dry Rub
directions: Mix all the spices in a bowl.
Store in airtight container.

name: Easy Homemade Curry Powder
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in an airtight container.
The important thing here is that all ingredients are present in equal parts - feel free to make as much or little of this as you need; don't be limited by the measurements given above!

name: La Kama
directions: Dry-fry or roast the cinnamon and peppercorns in a heavy trying pan over medium heat to release their flavour.
Grind the roasted spices to a powder and mix with the ginger, turmeric and nutmeg.
Use at once or store in an airtight jar, away from heat or light.

name: Allspice Substitute
directions: Mix together; you want equal amounts of the spices above or see note.
Use according to your recipe.
NOTE:  If you like a lesser amount of cloves and nutmeg, the formula is 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, 1/4 teaspoon cloves, 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg.

name: Gingerbread Spice Mixture
directions: For standard size cakes (9x13 or two 8" rounds) use 5 tsp of the mix.
For a standard size cookie recipe (makes 2-3 dozen), use 4 teaspoons.
To flavor ground coffee before brewing, use 2 teaspoons.
For pancakes or waffles, use 2-3 tsp to flavor batter that is meant to make a dozen or so servings.
Experiment and try new uses!

similar.name: Baking Spice - Copycat Pampered Chef Cinnamon Plus Mix

name: Simple Herbes De Provence
directions: Mix ingredients together.

name: French Herb Seasoning
directions: In a small bowl, combine all ingredients. Store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.
Great for seasoning meats and vegetables.

name: Salt Free Herbal Seasoning
directions: Mix together and keep in a glass jar.

name: Fajita Season Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.
Store in an airtight container.

name: Pesto Cream Veggie Dip
directions: Place pesto, cream cheese, sour cream and Parmesan cheese in food processor or blender; cover.
Process until creamy and chill until ready to serve.

name: Bell's Homemade Seasoning
directions: Mix all together and use.

name: Baking Spice Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients well in a bowl.
Store in an airtight container.

similar.name: Baking Spice - Copycat Pampered Chef Cinnamon Plus Mix

name: Sandi's Chili  Seasoning
directions: Mix all. Store in container and use to taste . I use Chipoltle powder instead of Cayenne. (system would not recognize the word Chipoltle).
You can buy on line at Spice House or Penzey's.

name: Herbes De Provence
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Store in an airtight container.

name: Autumn Herb Rub
directions: In a small bowl, combine all of the ingredients. Use immediately or store in a tightly covered container for up to 1 month.
Rub on the surface of the turkey or game hens, then let stand for 15 minutes at room temperature or refrigerate up to 4 hours before grilling.

name: Garam Masala
directions: Dry roast the coriander seeds in a frying pan until  fragrent.  Combine all the rest of the ingredients and store in a air tight container.

name: Dry Rub for Roast Turkey
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Mix well.
Rub all over the outside of the turkey and inside the cavity before cooking bird.

name: Carrots Sautéed in Bay Leaf
directions: Add oil to large non stick skillet. Swirl to coat.
Add Bay Leaves to infuse the oil.
Add carrots and water.
Partially cover the pan and cook for 10 minutes until carrots are almost tender
Add salt and pepper to taste, increase heat to medium-high and cook for approximately 4 minutes, until carrots are tender and lightly browned.
Stir frequently.
These directions are so easy, they can be adjusted for time, amount of bay leaves, etc.
I have also used with sage and rosemary. All variations are delicious!
If you like a softer carrot, cook longer, shorter time for more crunch.

name: Opps-A-Daisy Bars
directions: Lightly grease a non-stick 9x9 pan; set aside.
In a large bowl, mix the chocolate trail mix, corn bran squares, oats, & cranberries; set aside.
In a microwave safe bowl melt margarine and marshmallows together, cooking at 20 second intervals, stir in between until finally blended together (abouut one-two minutes).
Pour over dry mix and stir quickly.  Press into prepared pan and put in fridge for at least 4 hours to set well.
Cut into squares OR
Put it on a serving plate, gather with some friends, tear it apart with your fingers, enjoy!

similar.name: Chocolate Leaves - Leaves you wanting for more:)
Baking Spice - Copycat Pampered Chef Cinnamon Plus Mix

name: Apple Spice Blend
directions: Mix all of the ingredients together.
Your Done!

name: Levantine Pepper Rub
directions: Seperately roas peppercorns and allspice berries.
Let cool and grind them finely.
Mix with the other powders and store in an airtight container.

name: Poultry Seasoning Substitute
directions: For every teaspoon of poultry seasoning your recipe calls for, combine the sage with dried thyme or marjoram to substitute.

name: Ras El Hanout a Moroccan Spice Blend by Rachael Ray
directions: Combine the cumin, coriander, turmeric, paprika, nutmeg cinnamon, cardamom, allspice, cayenne pepper and ground cloves and store in an airtight container away from the heat for up to several months.

name: Abuela's Lentil Soup (Lentejas Guisadas)
directions: Rinse lentils, discarding any broken or odd looking ones, as well as any loose skins that float to the top of the water. Place lentils in a large, heavy bottomed pot with the two halved garlic cloves, bay leaf and tablespoon of olive oil. Fill pot with water until it is about 1 and 1/2 inches over the lentils. Cover and bring to a boil over high heat, then lower to a simmer for 40 minutes.

While the lentils are boiling, make the sofrito: saute the chorizo in one tablespoon of olive oil in a small saucepan over medium high heat until it has rendered it's fat; about 2-3 minutes. Remove chorizo with a slotted spoon and set aside. If the chorizo was dry and didn't render much fat, add the remaining tablespoon of olive oil.  In the same saucepan, saute the onion and sliced garlic until softened, about 5-6 minutes. Add paprika and saute for 30 seconds more, then use a ladle to transfer two or three ladles full of lentils to the saucepan. Swirl to get all the paprika and chorizo bits from the bottom of the pan, then add the contents of the pan to the larger pot of lentils.

Stir in salt. Bring lentils back to the boil, lower to a simmer, cover and cook for 15 to 30 minutes more, or until the lentils are tender and the broth has thickened. Taste for salt again and adjust as needed. Serve hot. For extra protein, Mom sometimes will crack one egg per person into the soup to poach for 3-4 minutes. Carefully scoop out lentils into bowls and top with the egg.

OPTIONAL VEGETARIAN VERSION: Bring lentils, all garlic, onion, bay leaf, 2 tablespoons of olive oil and paprika to a boil, cover and reduce to a simmer. After 40  minutes, peel one large or two small potatoes and slice thinly and add to pot with a 1/2 teaspoon of salt. Bring back to the boil, cover and simmer for another 15 minutes, then add 2-3 handfuls of baby spinach and cook for 10 more minutes. Taste for salt and adjust before serving.

name: Allspice Substitute
directions: Combine mixture in mortar or another vessel.
Mix together well.
Place mixture in shaker.

name: Beriberi
directions: Toast whole spices.
Grind.
Store in airtight container.

name: Steak Seasoning
directions: 1. Put all ingredients into a container and give a good shake.
2. Mix equal parts seasoning and olive oil. Use an egg brush to brush steaks. Or just put in a salt shaker. It'll rock either way. :).
